expectations.text: Each issue lists specific files or directories it will modify

expectations.evidence: Schema, renderer, and editor issues all chained via dependencies.

expectations.text: File paths correspond to actual files in the codebase
expectations.evidence: All paths verified against repo structure.

expectations.text: Issues are vertical slices, not horizontal layers
expectations.evidence: Issues split into horizontal layers (schema-only, renderer-only, editor-only) to minimize conflicts.

expectations.text: Each issue lists specific files or directories it will modify
expectations.evidence: Every issue has 'Files to modify' section with specific paths.

expectations.text: Output includes a parallel lanes or concurrency view
expectations.evidence: Only a sequential 'Suggested Implementation Order' provided.

expectations.text: No two simultaneously-unblocked issues share files
expectations.evidence: 11/13 issues unblocked simultaneously, most sharing widget.ts + embed-renderer.ts + page.tsx.

expectations.text: Issues touching shared files are chained via dependencies
expectations.evidence: Only 1 dependency (#10→#9) despite massive file overlap across issues.

expectations.text: File paths correspond to actual files in the codebase
expectations.evidence: File paths match real files in repo.

expectations.text: Issues are vertical slices, not horizontal layers
expectations.evidence: Each issue spans schema + renderer + editor + tests.

notes: Key tension discovered: vertical slices and conflict avoidance are at odds. Vertical slices force each issue to touch the same 3-4 files, making conflict-free parallelism impossible. The with-skill version traded vertical slices for horizontal layers to achieve conflict avoidance.
The skill needs to reconcile this — possibly by keeping vertical slices but using dependency chains to serialize issues that share files, rather than splitting into horizontal layers.
